parsed_review.summary: CP00-928 is a descriptor-only HRX-embedded bridge pack (10 implementation units, RP30.P04.M03.S06-RP30.P04.M04.S06: 4 AIReviewQueue tail rows + 6 AdminPolicy foundation rows). Verified via Read/Grep/Glob only. The HRX_CP928_PACK_BINDING (registry.js:610), required section rows (registry.js:2259), requirements, case-set, and descriptor generators are internally consistent and match the manifest plan_binding_snapshot and the live closeout-pack-plan.json. All descriptor-only boundaries hold: every runtime/record-write/review-action/rule-execution/policy-finalization/final-judgment flag is false, and no real employee/candidate/payroll data or credentials/secrets are present — enforced in createHrxDomainRows and the validateHrxCp928AiReviewAdminPolicyBridgeCoverage/Descriptor validators (validators.js:5433/5472) and contract validator (validators.js:5588). Tests (model.test.js:1662-1737) assert binding, coverage distributions, descriptor closure (runtime_opened/final_judgment_allowed/rule_execution_opened=false, emits_runtime_receipt=false), and contract projection currency. The committed contracts/hrx-people-contract.json is correctly regenerated for CP00-928 (latest_pack/latest_projection=HrxCp928AiReviewAdminPolicyBridgeDescriptor, case set 2 sections/10 rows, validation.valid=true). README and package.json script wiring (full validation ladder + hrx tests in npm test) are present. Claude remains a read-only independent reviewer (claude_is_final_approval=false, claude_packet.read_only=true, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false); Hermes is used only as a deterministic review/evidence packet (emits_runtime_receipt=false), not product runtime. The pack honestly reports production_ready=false / status=in_progress pending this review; its scoped descriptor_verified claim is fully supported by the implementation, tests, and local-validation wiring. The final validation ladder, adjudication, construction inspection, and human enterprise-trust approval remain expected post-review steps and are correctly left open (the pack makes no runtime or enterprise-trust claim). No P0/P1/P2 findings; one non-blocking P3 informational note.

findings.title: Contract JSON nested projection is spot-checked, not deep-diffed against the deterministic generator
findings.details: validateHrxPeopleContract regenerates the CP928 descriptor from code (validateHrxCp928AiReviewAdminPolicyBridgeDescriptor(undefined, contract)) and validates that generated object, while only asserting a few top-level fields of the committed contracts/hrx-people-contract.json (schema_version, program_contract, latest_pack.pack_id, latest_projection.descriptor, validation.valid). The deeply nested stored rows under latest_projection and projections.cp928 in the ~57k-line JSON are not reconciled byte-for-byte against the generator, and the contract test in model.test.js:1706 likewise only checks top-level fields. A stale or hand-edited nested region of the committed artifact would therefore not be caught by rp30:hrx:validate alone. Spot-checks during this review confirm the JSON is correctly regenerated for CP00-928 (latest_pack CP00-928 at line 64, latest_projection HrxCp928AiReviewAdminPolicyBridgeDescriptor at line 595, case set 2 sections/10 rows at line 648, validation.valid=true at line 57035), so there is no actual divergence in this pack.
findings.recommendation: Optional and non-blocking: consider asserting deep equality between createHrxPeopleContractProjection() output and the committed contracts/hrx-people-contract.json in a test or in validate-rp30-hrx-people-contract.mjs to detect drift in the stored artifact. This is a pre-existing pattern across all HRX packs (CP897-CP928), not introduced by CP00-928, and is not required for this closeout.
